decks.notes: This deck focuses on manipulating the graveyard with cards like Street Wraith and Generous Ent, while also utilizing cascade mechanics with cards like Shardless Agent to power out Living End and reanimate powerful creatures. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a large board presence and disrupt their game plan with cards like Sink into Stupor and Force of Negation. The inclusion of cards like Endurance and Subtlety provides additional ways to interact with the opponent's strategy. Ultimately, the goal is to set up a powerful Living End combo to swing the game in your favor.

This deck focuses on aggressive strategies combined with blinking effects to maximize value from entering the battlefield triggers. With cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler, you can repeatedly blink powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury. This allows you to apply pressure on your opponent while disrupting their game plan. The deck also includes removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Prismatic Ending to control the board. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and disruptive plays.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays, burn spells, and combat tricks to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes powerful creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, supported by cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer. The deck also includes Goblin Bombardment for direct damage and Thraben Charm for additional utility. The mana base is flexible with fetch lands and dual lands, as well as a singleton Blood Moon for disruption. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain momentum throughout the game.

The deck revolves around quickly reanimating powerful creatures from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. This combo-oriented strategy focuses on cheating big threats like Griselbrand onto the battlefield early in the game. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize to disrupt opponents' game plans. Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Fallaji Archaeologist provide additional utility and value to the deck, while cards like Solitude and Prismatic Ending help deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to win through explosive plays and overwhelming opponents with its instant reanimation abilities.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Amulet of Vigor, Primeval Titan, and lands like Crumbling Vestige and Simic Growth Chamber to efficiently cast big threats like Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. The deck also utilizes cards like Scapeshift and Urza's Saga to search for key pieces and Boseiju, Who Endures to protect critical spells. The goal is to assemble powerful combinations of lands and spells to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. Additionally, the deck includes utility lands like Mirrorpool and Hanweir Battlements for added versatility.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with hand disruption, counterspells, removal spells, and powerful creatures while generating card advantage through cards like Thoughtseize, Subtlety, Counterspell, and Tasigur, the Golden Fang. The deck aims to control the game with a combination of permission spells and removal, gradually wearing down the opponent's resources with powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Orcish Bowmasters. The deck also utilizes graveyard recursion and card draw to ensure longevity in the game, ultimately winning through incremental advantage and outvaluing the opponent.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through various counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to efficiently deal with threats using cards like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict, while also countering key spells with Spell Snare and Counterspell. Additionally, it focuses on card advantage and selection through cards like Snapcaster Mage, Cling to Dust, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Overall, the deck is designed to maintain control of the game and out-value the opponent in the long run.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using cards that don't require lands to function. By utilizing cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Lotus Bloom alongside cards that disrupt the opponent's strategy and control the board, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm and defeat the opponent. The key strategy involves carefully timing and sequencing the cards used to ensure the combo can be executed successfully, while also being prepared to counter or disrupt the opponent's moves. The deck emphasizes versatility, synergy, and fast-paced gameplay to achieve victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Amulet of Vigor to quickly ramp up mana and play large threats like Primeval Titan. The main combo involves using lands like Crumbling Vestige and Lotus Field, along with spell cards like Scapeshift, to search for specific lands and win conditions. Other key cards, such as Green Sun's Zenith and Urza's Saga, help support the combo strategy by tutoring for important pieces. The deck aims to assemble the necessary components quickly to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and spells.

The deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, using a combination of low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly defeat the opponent. Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer provide early pressure, while cards like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm offer removal and reach. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for additional threats. Overall, the Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creatures, burn spells, and planeswalker value to secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently through rituals and medallions, while digging through the deck with Impulse and Wrenn's Resolve to find combo pieces such as Past in Flames and Grapeshot. The goal is to cast a high storm count spell chain, using Past in Flames to recast rituals and spells from the graveyard, eventually finishing off the opponent with a lethal Grapeshot. The deck aims to win the game in explosive fashion, overwhelming the opponent with a storm of spells and burn damage.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively play creatures like Guide of Souls, Ocelot Pride, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, while also utilizing powerful cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. The deck aims to deal damage to the opponent efficiently with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge, and control the battlefield with Arena of Glory and Elegant Parlor. It also includes disruption with Thraben Charm and protection with Voice of Victory. The inclusion of cards like Sacred Foundry, Blood Moon, and The Legend of Roku adds additional utility to the deck.

This deck focuses on utilizing both aggressive creatures and blink effects to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By constantly blinking creatures like Flickerwisp and Solitude, the deck can remove threats and generate value. The inclusion of powerful cards like Thoughtseize and Teferi, Time Raveler adds disruption and control elements to the aggro strategy. Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler provide card advantage and potentially lock down the opponent's resources. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan through blink effects and disruption spells.

This deck revolves around controlling the game by utilizing a combination of powerful control spells and creatures. The key focus is on playing high-cost spells like Scion of Draco and Leyline of the Guildpact, supported by card draw engines like Helga, Skittish Seer and Quantum Riddler. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by generating card advantage and disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Negation. Overall, the strategy is to establish board control and overwhelm opponents with powerful late-game threats while maintaining a defensive position.

The deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Rider combo with Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to quickly tutor for powerful creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant and Griselbrand. It also includes protection cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The use of Ureni, the Song Unending and Consign to Memory allows for additional flexibility and card advantage. Overall, the main strategy is to efficiently ramp into big threats while utilizing various combo pieces to maintain control and momentum throughout the game.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures, backed up by efficient removal spells and cantrips to maintain card advantage and consistency. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, the deck aims to put constant pressure on the opponent while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The inclusion of cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration help to dig for key cards, allowing the deck to close out games quickly.
